description_long: This object is a single head of broccoli, featuring a thick, pale green stalk that transitions into a dense, rounded crown of tightly clustered green florets. The crown displays a bumpy, granular texture with subtle color variations from dark to light green, and the stalk shows fibrous striations and a slightly rough, uneven cut at the base. The branching structure is visible where the stalk splits into several smaller stems supporting the floret clusters. There are no attached leaves, and the overall form is asymmetrical, with the crown extending more on one side. The surface of the florets is matte and rough, while the stalk appears slightly smoother but still fibrous. The object is compact and solid, typical of fresh broccoli found in grocery stores or urban markets.
description: A dense, green broccoli head with a thick, fibrous stalk and tightly packed florets forming a rounded crown. The surface is rough and matte, with visible branching and no attached leaves.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thick stalk and the rounded, dense crown of green florets, with visible branching from the stalk.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the bumpy, tightly clustered florets and the overall circular shape of the crown.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the stalk and the asymmetrical extension of the crown, with branching structure visible.
description_view_3: Bottom view focusing on the cut end of the stalk, showing fibrous texture and the base of the floret branches.
attribute_floret_clustering: Florets are densely packed across the crown, with minor variation in size and tightness; some areas show slightly looser clustering near the edges.
attribute_stalk_segmentation: Stalk appears mostly continuous with subtle segmentation; a few minor nodes are visible where branches diverge, but no clear sectioning.
attribute_branching_pattern: Branching is irregular and asymmetrical, with several primary branches extending from the main stalk and supporting secondary floret clusters.
attribute_leaf_attachment: No leaves are attached; the stalk and branches are bare, with no visible leaf remnants at the base or along the stalk.
attribute_crown_density: Crown is solid and highly dense, with minimal gaps or open areas between the floret clusters.
